content.title: Footers, Piers & Structural Support
content.subtitle: Code-compliant concrete footings, piers, and structural support built to last. Maxima Concrete engineers strength, stability, and peace of mind. Free estimate.

content.title: Why foundations matter
content.subtitle: A strong foundation is essential to the success of any project. Maxima Concrete provides expertly built footings, piers, and structural support systems designed to ensure stability, durability, and long-term performance for residential and commercial applications.
content.paragraph1: Concrete footings and piers provide the structural support needed to safely transfer weight into the ground. They help prevent settling, shifting, and structural issues while creating a stable base for patios, retaining walls, garages, pergolas, and other concrete structures.
content.paragraph2: At Maxima Concrete, we build every foundation component to meet local codes and site requirements. Using quality materials, proper reinforcement, and proven construction methods, we deliver dependable support that helps protect your investment for years to come.

benefits.title: Concrete Footings (Footers) — Your load-spreading anchors
checkItems: Patio edges on slopes where the patio face is tall
benefits.description: Footings distribute structural loads into the soil, preventing settlement. We size them to code and conditions (load, soil bearing, frost depth), perfect for:

benefits.title: Concrete Piers — Vertical strength where you need it
benefits.description: Piers transfer loads upward from footings, ideal when slopes or point loads demand extra support:

benefits.title: Steel Reinforcement — The invisible safety net
benefits.description: We place rebar and mesh per plan/spec to resist tension, control cracking, and ensure rock-solid performance for decades.

styles.title: Broom-Finished:
styles.description: Broom-finished concrete is one of the most popular and versatile concrete finishes for patios, driveways, sidewalks, porches, and steps. Created by brushing the surface before it fully cures, this finish adds subtle texture and improved slip resistance while maintaining a clean, classic appearance. Durable, low-maintenance, and cost-effective, broom-finished concrete is ideal for both residential and commercial properties, offering long-lasting performance and timeless curb appeal.

styles.title: Stamped & Colored:
styles.description: Stamped and colored concrete enhances the look of patios, walkways, driveways, and outdoor living spaces. By imprinting patterns and adding color during the installation process, concrete can replicate the appearance of natural stone, brick, slate, or wood at a more cost-effective price. With a wide variety of textures, patterns, and color options available, this finish offers endless design possibilities. The result is a durable, low-maintenance surface that combines beauty and functionality.

styles.title: Exposed Aggregate:
styles.description: Exposed Aggregate concrete creates a bold, textured appearance by revealing the decorative stones embedded within the concrete surface. This finish highlights the natural color and character of the aggregate, creating added visual interest and depth. Popular for driveways, patios, pool decks, and walkways, Exposed Aggregate offers a durable, slip-resistant surface with a natural look.

styles.title: Buff Wash:
styles.description: Buff Wash concrete creates a unique, textured appearance by gently exposing the natural sand and fine aggregates within the concrete surface. Through a specialized finishing process, the top layer of concrete is carefully removed to reveal subtle textures and natural color variations. Available in different exposure levels, Buff Wash is a popular choice for patios, pool decks, walkways, and outdoor living areas, offering a refined, natural look with added texture and visual depth.

items.question: What is a concrete footer?
items.answer: A footer is the reinforced concrete base that supports foundations, walls, and columns, spreading the load and keeping structures stable.

items.question: How deep should footers be?
items.answer: Depth depends on the structure and the frost line. In Ohio, footers are placed below the frost line to prevent heaving, and we always build to local code.

items.question: Do you handle permits and inspections for footers?
items.answer: Yes. We manage the required permits and coordinate inspections so everything meets code from the start.

items.question: Do you offer free consultations?
items.answer: Yes — we provide free on-site consultations and estimates anywhere in Central Ohio.
